description.p: You can control tools you imbue with cursed energy.

sheet.description: You can control tools you imbue with cursed energy.

description.p: Beginning at 1st level, you can spend CE up to your CE modifier as a bonus action to imbue a tool you are holding with cursed energy. This imbuement lasts an hour for each cursed energy spent and gains the following benefits:

ul.li: You gain a +2 bonus to attack rolls using this tool or a +1 bonus to AC if the tool is armor or used defensively
The damage die increases by 1 tier and again at 6th and 11th level (D4-D6-D8-D10-2d6-2d8)

sheet.description: For up to {{ce:modifier}} CE, imbue a tool for an hour for each cursed energy spent, giving you a +2 to attack rolls or a +1 to AC if used defensively and increasing its damage die by 1.

For up to {{ce:modifier}} CE, imbue a tool for an hour for each cursed energy spent, giving you a +2 to attack rolls or a +1 to AC if used defensively and increasing its damage die by 2. You gain a +1 to attack and damage rolls for each CE spent.

For up to {{ce:modifier}} CE, imbue a tool for an hour for each cursed energy spent, giving you a +2 to attack rolls or a +1 to AC if used defensively and increasing its damage die by 3. You gain a +1 to attack and damage rolls for each CE spent. When you or a creature you can see makes an attack using your imbued tool, you can use your reaction to grant that attack roll advantage.

description.p: At 3rd level, when you imbue a tool, you can spend an additional 2 CE to create a Floating Tool. You can command a Floating Tool as an action to hover up to 35 feet in any direction and make a melee attack using your CE modifier. The Floating Tool deals its normal weapon damage or 1d4 bludgeoning, slashing, or piercing if it is an improvised weapon. You can only have one floating tool at a time and it falls to the ground if it is more than 120 ft from you.

A Floating Tool has AC equal to your CE save DC and hit points equal to 5 x your proficiency bonus + 10 for each grade above 4th if it is a cursed tool. A Floating Tool reduced to 0 HP is destroyed unless it is a cursed tool, in which case it loses the benefits of Floating Tool. You can spend 2 CE as a bonus action to return the effect of Floating Tool to the cursed tool by touching it.

sheet.description: When you imbue a tool, you can spend an additional 2 CE to create a Floating Tool. You can command a Floating Tool as an action to hover up to 35 feet in any direction and make a melee attack using your CE modifier. The Floating Tool deals its normal weapon damage or 1d4 bludgeoning, slashing, or piercing if it is an improvised weapon. You can only have one floating tool at a time and it falls to the ground if it is more than 120 ft from you. A Floating Tool has a AC of {{ce:dc:total}} and {{tool:manipulation:floating:tool:hp}} hit points for each grade above 4th if it is a cursed tool. A Floating Tool reduced to 0 HP is destroyed unless it is a cursed tool, in which case it loses the benefits of Floating Tool. You can spend 2 CE as a bonus action to return the effect of Floating Tool to the cursed tool by touching it.

When you imbue a tool, you can spend an additional 1 CE to create a Floating Tool. You can command 2 Floating Tools as an action to hover up to 40 feet in any direction and make a melee attack using your CE modifier. The Floating Tool deals its normal weapon damage or 1d4 bludgeoning, slashing, or piercing if it is an improvised weapon. You can have two floating tools at a time and it falls to the ground if it is more than 120 ft from you. A Floating Tool has a AC of {{ce:dc:total}} and {{tool:manipulation:floating:tool:hp}} hit points for each grade above 4th if it is a cursed tool. You gain a flying speed of 60 feet by mounting a Floating Tool. A Floating Tool reduced to 0 HP is destroyed unless it is a cursed tool, in which case it loses the benefits of Floating Tool. You can spend 2 CE as a bonus action to return the effect of Floating Tool to the cursed tool by touching it.

When you imbue a tool, you can spend an additional 1 CE to create a Floating Tool. You can command 2 Floating Tools as an bonus action or 1 as a free action to hover up to 40 feet in any direction and make a melee attack using your CE modifier. The Floating Tool deals its normal weapon damage or 1d4 bludgeoning, slashing, or piercing if it is an improvised weapon. You can have two floating tools at a time and it falls to the ground if it is more than 120 ft from you. A Floating Tool has a AC of {{ce:dc:total}} and {{tool:manipulation:floating:tool:hp}} hit points for each grade above 4th if it is a cursed tool. You gain a flying speed of 60 feet by mounting a Floating Tool and creatures on Floating Tools gain advantage on Dexterity saves. A Floating Tool reduced to 0 HP is destroyed unless it is a cursed tool, in which case it loses the benefits of Floating Tool. You can spend 2 CE as a bonus action to return the effect of Floating Tool to the cursed tool by touching it.

description.p: At 6th level you have learned to use tools offensively. As an action for 3 CE, you can create a blast of wind from an imbued tool you are holding. Or from a Floating Tool within 60 ft for 1 additional CE.

This creates a 10 feet wide, 60 feet long line. Creatures within range must make a Strength saving throw, taking 3d10 bludgeoning damage and pushed back 10 feet on a failed save or half as much on a success.
At 11th level, your Wind Scythe now deals 6d10 bludgeoning damage and a creature that fails their save is pushed back 20 feet and knocked prone.
Additionally, your specialty grants you the following Tool Improvements.

ul.li: The cost to create a Floating Tool becomes 1 CE
When you command a Floating Tool, they can move up to 40 feet
You gain a flying speed of 60 feet by mounting a Floating Tool
You can have 2 Floating Tools active at the same time, commanding both as part of the same action.

description.p: You gain a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls when using an imbued tool for each CE spent.

As a reaction, you can cause one of your imbued tools to protect you. It grants you a bonus to AC or reduces damage depending on its size. If the tool has a bonus to AC or damage rolls, it is included in the AC and reduction respectively.

sheet.description: For 3 CE, create a 10 feet wide, 60 feet long line from an imbued tool you are holding or a Floating Tool within 60 ft for 1 additional CE. Creatures within range must make a Strength saving throw, taking 3d10 bludgeoning damage and being pushed back 10 feet on a failed save or half as much on a success.

For 3 CE, create a 10 feet wide, 60 feet long line from an imbued tool you are holding or a Floating Tool within 60 ft for 1 additional CE. Creatures within range must make a Strength saving throw, taking 6d10 bludgeoning damage and being pushed back 20 feet and knocked prone on a failed save or half as much on a success.

description.p: You gain a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls when using an imbued tool for each CE spent.

As a reaction, you can cause one of your imbued tools to protect you. It grants you a bonus to AC or reduces damage depending on its size. If the tool has a bonus to AC or damage rolls, it is included in the AC and reduction respectively.

sheet.description: Cause one of your tools to give you a +1, +2, +3, +4, or +5 to AC and a 1d4, 2d4, 2d6, 2d8, or 2d10 damage reduction to incoming damage for sizes tiny, small, medium, large, or huge sizes respectively. Bonuses to AC or damage rolls are included in the AC and reduction respectively.

Cause one of your tools to give you a +2, +3, +4, +5, or +6 to AC and a 2d4, 3d4, 3d6, 3d8, or 3d10 damage reduction to incoming damage for sizes tiny, small, medium, large, or huge sizes respectively. Bonuses to AC or damage rolls are included in the AC and reduction respectively. You have one extra reaction each round that can only be used for this technique.

description.p: At 11th level, you have mastered your respective style, granting you the following:

Floating Tool Mastery. You can command two Floating Tools using your bonus action or one Floating Tool as a free action on your turn. You and other creatures also gain advantage on Dexterity saves while riding a Floating Tool.

Combat Tool Mastery. Your mastery over imbued tools allows you to manipulate the flow of combat with exceptional precision. When you use Combat Tool Improvement, the AC bonus it grants increases by 1, and the damage reduction provided increases by one additional die. In addition, you gain one extra reaction each round that can only be used to activate Combat Tool Improvement.
When you or a creature you can see makes an attack using your imbued tool, you can use your reaction to grant that attack roll advantage.

Finally at 18th level you have learned to manifest your domain. All weapon attacks made by creatures other than you become a guaranteed miss as you can manipulate all tools within your domain, causing them to come to life and obey your commands.

At initiative 20, you choose one of your imbued tools to gain the benefits of Floating Tool and have all Floating Tools move 60 ft and attack a creature within range or have all creatures suffer the effects of Wind Scythe. You can control a number of Floating Tools equal to your CE modifier (min of 2) within your domain.

sheet.description: All weapon attacks made by creatures other than you become a guaranteed miss. At initiative 20, you choose one of your imbued tools to gain the benefits of Floating Tool and have all Floating Tools move 60 ft and attack a creature within range or have all creatures suffer the effects of Wind Scythe. You can control {{tool:manipulation:domain:tool:max}} Floating Tools within your domain.
